findings.title: Near-duplicate split-image pixel buffer generation across test files
findings.description: The horizontal-split and vertical-split image generation pattern appears in both phash.test.ts (lines 65-88) and screenshot-pipeline.test.ts (lines 17-50). Both create the same pair of images: left-half-black/right-half-white and top-half-black/bottom-half-white. The code is nearly identical, spanning ~30 lines in each file. This is a maintenance burden -- if the image generation logic needs to change (e.g., different dimensions for testing), both files must be updated in lockstep.

findings.title: StubVlmProvider duplicated across two test files
findings.description: StubVlmProvider is defined in both screenshot-processor.test.ts (lines 10-24) and screenshot-pipeline.test.ts (lines 70-81). The two implementations are slightly different (one accepts a configurable result, the other tracks callCount and returns a fixed result), but both serve the same purpose: stubbing the external VLM API boundary. Having two separate stubs means two places to update if VlmProvider changes.

findings.title: Magic number 5 for default pHash similarity threshold
findings.description: The default similarity threshold of 5 appears as a magic number in areSimilar() and is repeated as the default in ScreenshotPipelineOptions (screenshot-pipeline.ts line 35). While HASH_SIZE is properly named as a constant, the threshold default is not, making it less clear what '5' represents and creating duplication risk if the default needs to change.

positive_observations: All source files are well within size limits (largest is 109 lines). Functions are short with clear single responsibilities.
Named constant HASH_SIZE = 8 in phash.ts demonstrates good naming practice over magic numbers.

Clean separation of concerns: phash (pure/functional), ScreenshotCapture (imperative shell), ScreenshotProcessor (VLM coordination), ScreenshotPipeline (orchestration). Each has low cyclomatic complexity (max ~4).
Maximum nesting depth across all source files is 2 levels (loops in phash.ts). No deep nesting detected.
Guard clauses and early returns used consistently (start() returns early if timer exists, hammingDistance throws early on length mismatch).
Test Doubles properly documented with '// Test Double rationale:' comments explaining why each stub exists.
